title: Bradleia glochidion
intro: Bradleia glochidion, also known as the Bunga Raya or Fire Bush, is a flowering plant species native to the tropical regions of Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of fever, wounds, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Bradleia glochidion include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Bradleia glochidion is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is used to treat a range of conditions, including fever, skin conditions, and respiratory issues.
